m	Z	
base)
commandsc
VMPlugina|
	`vm`::
	Enables or disables transparent huge pages depending on value of the
	[option]`transparent_hugepages` option. The option can have one of three
	possible values `always`, `madvise` and `never`.
	.Disable transparent hugepages
	====
	----
	[vm]
	transparent_hugepages=never
	----
	====
	The [option]`transparent_hugepage.defrag` option specifies the
	defragmentation policy. Possible values for this option are `always`,
	`defer`, `defer+madvise`, `madvise` and `never`. For a detailed
	explanation of these values refer to
	link:https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/vm/transhuge.txt[Transparent Hugepage Support].
transparent_hugepages
transparent_hugepage
transparent_hugepage.defrag
selfr
;/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/tuned/plugins/plugin_vm.py
_get_config_options$
VMPlugin._get_config_optionsc
NTF)
_has_static_tuningZ
_has_dynamic_tuning
instancer
_instance_init,
VMPlugin._instance_initc
_instance_cleanup0
VMPlugin._instance_cleanupc
Nz#/sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepagez*/sys/kernel/mm/redhat_transparent_hugepage)
path
exists)
	_thp_path3
VMPlugin._thp_pathr
always
neverZ
madvisez-Incorrect 'transparent_hugepages' value '%s'.z

zWtransparent_hugepage is already set in kernel boot cmdline, ignoring value from profile
